ports.notes: Current version, ECDSA and ED25519 host keys
Redirects to http://facts.htb/. Proxies CamaleonCMS (Ruby on Rails). Images served from MinIO via /randomfacts/ path.
S3-compatible object storage API. Redirects to port 9001 (console) which is not externally accessible. Health endpoints respond 200.

anomalies.description: MinIO console (port 9001) referenced in redirect but not externally accessible. Port scan shows 65532 closed ports. Console likely bound to localhost only.
anomalies.significance: Console access would reveal version and admin interface. May be accessible from within the host after foothold.

anomalies.description: CamaleonCMS has open user registration at /admin/register with CAPTCHA protection
anomalies.significance: Critical — all known CamaleonCMS CVEs require authenticated access. Open registration provides that access vector.

anomalies.description: MinIO randomfacts bucket is publicly listable via S3 API on port 54321, but nginx blocks directory listing on port 80
anomalies.significance: Public bucket policy. Only contains image files. Owner DisplayName is 'minio' (default).

gaps.description: MinIO exact version unknown — no version header exposed. Need console access (post-foothold) or authenticated API call to determine version.
CamaleonCMS exact version unknown — need to confirm whether vulnerable versions are running. Asset digests suggest modern deployment.
Other MinIO buckets not enumerated — root listing denied. May contain sensitive data.

oracle_recommendations.action: Web enumeration of CamaleonCMS — identify version, enumerate paths, check for additional attack surface
oracle_recommendations.rationale: CamaleonCMS has three high-severity CVEs (2024-46986, 2024-46987, 2025-2304) all requiring auth. Registration is open. Version confirmation is critical.

oracle_recommendations.action: Register account on CamaleonCMS and test CVE-2025-2304 (mass assignment privesc)
oracle_recommendations.rationale: If CamaleonCMS version < 2.9.1, mass assignment during registration can escalate to admin. Admin access enables file write (RCE) and path traversal.

oracle_recommendations.action: Enumerate MinIO buckets via authenticated access or brute-force common bucket names
oracle_recommendations.rationale: MinIO may contain application secrets, backups, or credentials. Root listing denied but individual bucket names can be guessed.
